name: NSCLC OS link from the integrated tumor burden (log-burden AUC, Weibull PH)
description: Parametric proportional-hazards link from a TGI metric to population overall survival, driven by the INTEGRATED TUMOR BURDEN — the time-averaged log relative tumor size over the observation horizon (the AUC of the log-size curve; the log geometric-mean relative burden). S(t) = exp(-(t/scale)^shape * exp(beta * b)), b = mean_t log(v(t)/y0); a tumor that spends more time large (shallow nadir AND/OR a heavy regrowth tail) carries more burden and raises the hazard. Unlike the week-8 link (one early point, blind to the tail) and unlike the k_g link (the terminal regrowth slope, blind to depth), this single covariate integrates BOTH depth of response and regrowth, so it re-ranks models a third way. NON-DEFAULT (structure.default=false), reached via the explicit survival_link= argument, making 'which on-treatment metric predicts survival' an explicit model-selection axis. The covariate is horizon-dependent by construction (it is a cumulative-burden summary); a complete responder's burden is floored at the detection limit, not -inf. Population/trial-level only; values illustrative and unverified.

parameters.label: Weibull scale (median-OS scale at zero net burden)

extraction.tier_rationale: Sets baseline median OS for a tumor held at baseline size (zero integrated burden).

parameters.label: log-hazard coefficient on the integrated log-burden

extraction.tier_rationale: The integrated burden spans several log units across models; link strength varies across trials.

predictive_performance.population: independent NSCLC cohort (illustrative; an integrated-burden metric tends to sit between early-shrinkage and k_g on discrimination)
